extraction_summary.primary_workflow: User searches for a person in the Vue.js web client, selects a result and their payment recipient, then transfers all data to the legacy Allegro Swing desktop form via WebSocket relay.

business_rules.rule: Application must remain alive until explicitly closed by the user
business_rules.rationale: CountDownLatch with count=1 is never decremented from this entry point, preventing thread exit

business_rules.rule: WebSocket messages must contain a 'target' field indicating display destination ('textarea' or 'textfield')
business_rules.rationale: The onMessage handler performs a switch on message.target to route data to the correct UI control

business_rules.rule: When WebSocket message target is 'textarea', message content is displayed directly in the text area
business_rules.rationale: Separate routing path in onMessage for textarea target

business_rules.rule: When WebSocket message target is 'textfield', the full message JSON is parsed into individual form fields (name, first, dob, zip, ort, street, hausnr, iban, bic, valid_from)
business_rules.rationale: toSearchResult parses the JSON and maps each field to the corresponding JTextField

business_rules.rule: The WebSocket Swing client connects to the server at ws://localhost:1337/ on startup
business_rules.rationale: Hard-coded URI in main method; no configuration override

business_rules.rule: Gender selection defaults to Female (Weiblich) on initial form load
business_rules.rationale: rb_female.setSelected(true) is called during UI initialization in both Swing variants

business_rules.rule: All 13 domain fields must be initialized on model creation: TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE
business_rules.rationale: PocModel constructor adds all 13 ModelProperties with null ValueModel entries to the EnumMap

business_rules.rule: On form action, all 13 field values are converted to strings and submitted together as a single JSON payload
business_rules.rationale: PocModel.action() iterates all ModelProperties values and adds them to the data map for HTTP POST

business_rules.rule: A non-empty HTTP response triggers a model event to update the UI with the response data
business_rules.rationale: action() checks !responseBody.isEmpty() before emitting the response

business_rules.rule: An empty HTTP response triggers a 'Failed operation' event to notify the user of failure
business_rules.rationale: else branch in action() emits 'Failed operation' when responseBody is empty

business_rules.rule: Gender is stored as three separate Boolean fields (MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE) allowing exclusive selection tracking
business_rules.rationale: ModelProperties defines FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE as separate entries; ValueModel<Boolean> is used for each

business_rules.rule: Form data is submitted to the backend via HTTP POST to http://localhost:8080/post with Content-Type: application/json
business_rules.rationale: HttpBinService hardcodes URL=http://localhost:8080 and PATH=/post; sets Content-Type header explicitly

business_rules.rule: On receiving a response event, the text area displays the server response and ALL input fields are cleared to empty
business_rules.rationale: EventEmitter subscriber in PocPresenter sets all text fields to empty string and displays eventData in textArea

business_rules.rule: After receiving a response event, the gender selection is reset to Female (Weiblich) as the default
business_rules.rationale: Presenter event handler calls view.female.setSelected(true) and sets male/diverse to false

business_rules.rule: All text field changes (both insert and remove operations) are immediately propagated to the model
business_rules.rationale: DocumentListener insertUpdate and removeUpdate both call model.setField(content)

business_rules.rule: Radio button state changes are immediately propagated to the model as Boolean values
business_rules.rationale: ChangeListener on each radio button calls model.setField(source.isSelected())

business_rules.rule: The WebSocket relay server listens on port 1337
business_rules.rationale: webSocketsServerPort = 1337 is used for both HTTP server and WebSocket server

business_rules.rule: All incoming WebSocket connections are accepted regardless of origin (no origin validation)
business_rules.rationale: request.accept(null, request.origin) accepts any origin

business_rules.rule: Only UTF-8 text messages are processed and broadcast; binary messages are ignored
business_rules.rationale: message.type === 'utf8' guard before processing in the message handler

business_rules.rule: Messages are broadcast to ALL currently connected clients including the original sender
business_rules.rationale: Loop iterates clients array and sends to clients[i] without excluding the sender

business_rules.rule: Search is performed against a local in-memory dataset of 5 predefined persons
business_rules.rationale: search_space is a hardcoded array of 5 person objects in the component data()

business_rules.rule: Person search matches on ANY of the following criteria (OR logic): last name (partial, case-insensitive), first name (partial, case-insensitive), ZIP (exact match), city (partial, case-insensitive), street (partial, case-insensitive), house number (partial, case-insensitive)
business_rules.rationale: searchPerson() uses || operators; ZIP uses == (exact), all others use .toLowerCase().indexOf() (partial)

business_rules.rule: ZIP code matching is exact (not partial), unlike all other search fields which use partial matching
business_rules.rationale: element.zip == this.formdata.zip uses equality, while other fields use indexOf

business_rules.rule: A search criterion is only applied if the corresponding form field has a non-empty value (falsy check)
business_rules.rationale: Conditions check this.formdata.last && ... meaning empty fields are skipped

business_rules.rule: Each person record must have: customer number (knr), last name (name), first name (first), date of birth (dob), ZIP (zip), city (ort), street (street), house number (hausnr), and a zahlungsempfaenger array
business_rules.rationale: search_space dataset structure defines these as the required fields for display and search

business_rules.rule: Each Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipient) record must have: IBAN, BIC, valid_from date, valid_until date, and type fields
business_rules.rationale: zahlungsempfaenger array entries in search_space contain iban, bic, valid_from, valid_until, type

business_rules.rule: A person can have multiple Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipients) - between 1 and 3 in the mock dataset
business_rules.rationale: Mock data shows Hans Mayer with 2, Karl May with 3, others with 1-2 payment recipients

business_rules.rule: The 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen' (Transfer to Allegro) button sends the selected person and their selected Zahlungsempfaenger to the Swing client via WebSocket with target 'textfield'
business_rules.rationale: sendMessage(selected_result, 'textfield') is called on button click

business_rules.rule: When sending data to Allegro ('textfield' target), the zahlungsempfaenger array is replaced with the single selected Zahlungsempfaenger object
business_rules.rationale: sendMessage sets obj_to_send.zahlungsempfaenger = this.zahlungsempfaenger_selected before sending

business_rules.rule: The textarea content is automatically sent to the Swing client via WebSocket with target 'textarea' on every change (reactive watcher)
business_rules.rationale: Vue watch on internal_content_textarea calls sendMessage(val, 'textarea') on every change

business_rules.rule: WebSocket connection to ws://localhost:1337/ is established automatically on Vue component mount
business_rules.rationale: mounted() lifecycle hook calls this.connect()

business_rules.rule: Fields Kundennummer, IBAN, BIC, Betriebsbez, RV-Nummer, BG-Nummer, Traeger-Nr. are read-only (disabled) in the search form
business_rules.rationale: These inputs have the 'disabled' attribute in the template, preventing user input

business_rules.rule: The API endpoint POST /post accepts a user object with fields: FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, STREET, BIC, ORT, ZIP, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE, IBAN, VALID_FROM, TEXT_AREA
business_rules.rationale: OpenAPI 3.0 spec defines PostObject schema with these exact property names

business_rules.rule: The API response echoes back the PostObject containing all submitted field values
business_rules.rationale: PostResponseObject references PostObject in the json field, consistent with HTTPBin echo behavior

business_rules.rule: The API server runs on http://localhost:8080
business_rules.rationale: servers section in api.yml specifies url: http://localhost:8080

business_rules.rule: The data transfer message format for WebSocket is: {target: string, content: object} where target is 'textfield' or 'textarea'
business_rules.rationale: sendMessage serializes {target: target, content: obj_to_send}; extract() in Java parses this format

business_rules.rule: The JSON message parser in Java extracts 'content' field differently based on target: for 'textarea' target it uses the content string value; for other targets it uses the full raw JSON string
business_rules.rationale: In extract(), if target is 'textarea' strContent = jsonParser.getString(), else strContent = json (full raw JSON)

business_rules.rule: The application requires an HTTPBin Docker container running on port 8080 as a mock backend before starting
business_rules.rationale: README instructs: docker run -p 8080:80 kennethreitz/httpbin before running the app

business_rules.rule: The application requires Java SDK version 22.0.1 or higher
business_rules.rationale: Maven compiler plugin sets source=22 and target=22

business_rules.rule: The selected search result row is visually highlighted in the results table to indicate selection
business_rules.rationale: CSS class 'highlight' is conditionally applied when item.knr == selected_result.knr

business_rules.rule: The selected Zahlungsempfaenger row is visually highlighted in the payment table to indicate selection
business_rules.rationale: CSS class 'highlight' is applied when item.iban == zahlungsempfaenger_selected.iban

business_rules.rule: The search result table displays columns: Kdn.-nr (customer number), Name, Vorname (first name), Geburtsdatum (date of birth), PLZ (ZIP), Strasse (street + hausnr combined), Ort (city)
business_rules.rationale: Table header and data cells in the search_result div define these columns; street and hausnr are concatenated in display

workflows.workflow_name: Person Search and Transfer to Allegro
workflows.description: The primary end-to-end workflow where a user searches for a customer in the web client and transfers their data to the legacy Swing desktop form.

steps.action: User enters search criteria (name, first name, ZIP, city, street, or house number) in the Search form
User clicks 'Suchen' (Search) button
System filters in-memory dataset against all provided criteria using OR logic
System displays matching results in the search results table
User clicks a result row to select a person
System displays the selected person's Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipients) in the payment table
User clicks a Zahlungsempfaenger row to select a payment recipient
User clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen' button
System builds the transfer payload: deep-copies selected person, replaces zahlungsempfaenger array with single selected entry
System sends JSON message {target: 'textfield', content: personData} over WebSocket to relay server
Node.js relay server receives and broadcasts the message to all connected clients
Java Swing client receives the message and routes it to the 'textfield' handler
System parses the JSON payload and populates all form fields (name, first, dob, zip, ort, street, hausnr, iban, bic, valid_from)

workflows.description: The workflow where a user fills in the Swing MVP form and submits it to the HTTPBin backend.

steps.action: User enters data in the Swing form fields (first name, last name, DOB, address, IBAN, BIC, etc.)
Each keystroke immediately updates the corresponding model field via DocumentListener binding
User clicks 'Anordnen' (Submit) button
Presenter triggers model.action()
Model collects all 13 field values and submits them as JSON via HTTP POST to localhost:8080/post
Model receives the response from HTTPBin and evaluates if it is non-empty
Model emits the response body (or 'Failed operation') as an event via EventEmitter
Presenter receives the event and displays the response in the text area, clears all form fields, and resets gender to Female

workflows.description: A continuous background workflow that mirrors textarea content from the Vue.js client to the Swing client in real time.

steps.action: User types in the textarea in the Vue.js Search component
Vue watcher on internal_content_textarea fires immediately on every change
System sends {target: 'textarea', content: text} over WebSocket to relay server
Relay server broadcasts the message to all connected clients
Swing WebSocket client receives message, routes to textarea, and displays the content

fields: dob (date of birth, string, format YYYY-MM-DD)
zip (postal code, string, exact match in search)
content (object or string: the payload data)
